title: Isomer
short: One of two or more compounds with the same molecular formula but a different arrangement of atoms.
full:
Isomers share a molecular formula yet differ in structure, which gives them different properties. Structural (constitutional) isomers connect the same atoms in a different order — butane and 2-methylpropane are both C₄H₁₀ but one is a straight chain and the other branched.

Stereoisomers have the same connectivity but a different arrangement in space (for example cis/trans isomers across a double bond). The number of isomers grows quickly with the number of carbon atoms, which is why organic nomenclature must specify exactly where branches and functional groups sit.
